title: Invalid verilated code generated
body:
I'm running a testbench (via cocotb) with a parameterised data width. At 256 bits, all is well. At 512 bits, the generated code is invalid. I'm not going to be able to release the code, but i'll try and provide as much info as i can.

The error is this:

```console
INFO     cocotb:simulator.py:302 Vtop___024root__DepSet_heccd7ead__1.cpp: In function ‘void Vtop___024root___eval(Vtop___024root*)’:
INFO     cocotb:simulator.py:302 Vtop___024root__DepSet_heccd7ead__1.cpp:5334:5: error: ‘__Vilp’ was not declared in this scope
```

Looking at that line, we have this:

```cpp
void Vtop___024root___eval(Vtop___024root* vlSelf) {
    if (false && vlSelf) {}  // Prevent unused
    Vtop__Syms* const __restrict vlSymsp VL_ATTR_UNUSED = vlSelf->vlSymsp;
    VL_DEBUG_IF(VL_DBG_MSGF("+    Vtop___024root___eval\n"); );
    // Init
    __Vilp = 0U;
    while ((__Vilp <= 0x2aU)) {
         // I've changed signal names here
         vlSelf->__Vsampled__TOP__ip__DOT__ip1__DOT__m03_axis_f__DOT__axis_packed[__Vilp] 
            = vlSelf->ip__DOT__ip1__DOT__m03_axis_f__DOT__axis_packed[__Vilp];
        __Vilp = ((IData)(1U) + __Vilp);
    }
```
using grep -R __Vilp, I can find _nowhere_ in the generated verilated code which declares `__Vilp`

Comparing to the code in the 256 bit width version

```cpp
void Vtop___024root___eval(Vtop___024root* vlSelf) {
    if (false && vlSelf) {}  // Prevent unused
    Vtop__Syms* const __restrict vlSymsp VL_ATTR_UNUSED = vlSelf->vlSymsp;
    VL_DEBUG_IF(VL_DBG_MSGF("+    Vtop___024root___eval\n"); );
      // Init
      vlSelf->__Vsampled__TOP__ip__DOT__ip1__DOT__m03_axis_f__DOT__axis_packed[0u] 
            = vlSelf->ip__DOT__ip1__DOT__m03_axis_f__DOT__axis_packed[0u];
```

It looks like in this case, it does the initalisation with loop unrolling. It looks like in the none unrolled case, the variable needs declaring first? I'd guess the likely curprit is in here https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/c9d7486d16223e414b89e2545354245d3bec4788/src/V3Reloop.cpp#L24 , but i'm afraid I don't follow the internals here sufficiently well

I've tried this on verilator 5.010, and again at 5.020. I'm on ubuntu, but all the tools are packaged with nix

The command invoking verilator is below. I've stripped out an aweful lot of includes
```console
perl /nix/store/g352nrsp61alrgqsd2825wqbqfg82srb-verilator-5.020/bin/verilator -cc --exe \
    -Mdir /home/user/ip/sim_build/ip-4-1-512 \
    -DCOCOTB_SIM=1 \
    --top-module ip \
    --vpi \
    --public-flat-rw \
    --prefix Vtop \
    -o ip \
    -LDFLAGS -Wl,-rpath,/nix/store/bvcp6l3g4ac52gv3fjl8ig1dyfwwhczv-python3-3.9.13-env/lib/python3.9/site-packages/cocotb/libs \
    -L/nix/store/bvcp6l3g4ac52gv3fjl8ig1dyfwwhczv-python3-3.9.13-env/lib/python3.9/site-packages/cocotb/libs \
    -lcocotbvpi_verilator \
    -I/home/user/ip0 \
    -I/home/user/ip1 \
    -I/home/user/ip2 \
   -DOBSOLETE -DONESPIN \
   -Wno-fatal \
   --assert -DFORMAL \
   --coverage-line \
    -j 0 \
   -O3 \
   -CFLAGS -O3 \
   --threads 24 \
   -GDATA=512 \
   -GID=1 \
   -GDEST=4 \
   /nix/store/bvcp6l3g4ac52gv3fjl8ig1dyfwwhczv-python3-3.9.13-env/lib/python3.9/site-packages/cocotb/share/lib/verilator/verilator.cpp \
   .verilator.vlt \
   /home/user/ip/ip.sv
```
